originalTitle: ‘My heart burns with pain’: Iranian mother tells UN of Minab school attack
title: News from Our World: Keeping All Children Safe!
content:
Hello, Little Stars!

Hello, my little stars and sunbeams! It's me, your friendly news reporter for Africa, here to share something important with you today. We love to learn about our world, don't we? We love hearing about new animals, amazing places, and clever inventions. Sometimes news is happy, and sometimes it makes us a little sad, but it's always good to learn and understand. Today, we're going to talk about something a little bit serious, but it's very important. It's about keeping children safe, just like you! It shows us that even when sad things happen, people care and try to help. Did you know? Every child in the world is special, like a tiny shining star!

What is a School?

Do you go to school? School is a wonderful place, isn't it? It's where you learn new things like reading, writing, and counting. You play with your friends, draw colourful pictures, and maybe even sing songs and dance! Schools are supposed to be very safe places. They are like big, happy homes where teachers help you grow and learn new things every day. Everyone wants schools to be safe and full of laughter and fun. It's a place where you can make friends and discover exciting ideas. Learning at school helps you grow up strong and smart. That's why keeping schools safe is so, so important for everyone. Did you know? Many children in Africa love going to school to learn and play, just like you! They carry their books and smiles to school every day.

A Sad Day in Minab

But sometimes, sad things can happen in our world. Far, far away, in a place called Minab, there was a school. One day, something very, very sad happened there. It wasn't a happy day, and it made many people very, very sad, especially mothers and fathers. A kind mother named Mohaddeseh Fallahat had two children. Her children loved going to school, just like you do. They liked to learn and play with their friends. But on that sad day, her children were hurt very badly, and they are not with us anymore. Can you imagine how sad that would make a mother feel? Her heart was very, very sore. She missed her children so much, and her heart felt like it was burning with sadness. This sad event showed everyone how important it is for schools to be super safe places. Did you know? It's okay to feel sad when bad things happen. Everyone feels sad sometimes, and it's good to talk about it with a grown-up.

A Mother's Love

This brave mother, Mohaddeseh Fallahat, loved her children more than anything in the whole wide world. When something so sad happens, sometimes people want to tell others about it. They want to share their story so that it doesn't happen again. She wanted to tell everyone that schools must always, always be safe. She wanted to share her very sad story so that no other children or mothers would ever have to feel this terrible sadness. She wanted to protect other little stars like you. Her biggest wish was for every school, everywhere, to be a happy, safe place. She wanted classrooms to be filled with laughter and learning, not sadness or worry. Her love for her children made her very strong and brave. Did you know? A mother's love is one of the strongest and most wonderful things in the world! It can help mothers be very brave.

Big Helpers: The United Nations

So, this brave mother decided to go to a very important place. It's called the United Nations, or the UN for short. Imagine a very, very big meeting hall, where people from almost every country in the world come together. It's like a big family meeting for the whole world! These people are like big, important helpers. They talk about ways to make our world better and safer for everyone. They talk about making peace, making sure everyone has enough food, and helping sick people. And most importantly, they talk about keeping children safe and happy! They want to make sure all kids can live good lives. The UN has a special group called the Human Rights Council. These people listen to stories from all over the world. They listen to make sure everyone is treated fairly and safely, especially children. They are there to protect people's rights. Did you know? The United Nations was started after big wars, so people could talk instead of fight and help each other make the world more peaceful!

Listening and Helping Hands

Mohaddeseh Fallahat stood up in front of all these important people from different countries. She told them her story. She told them how much she missed her children and how important it is for all schools to be safe places. She spoke with a brave voice, even though her heart was still aching with sadness. Another kind person from her country, Mr. Araghchi, was also there to speak to the UN. He also talked about how incredibly important it is to protect children. He also wanted to make sure that schools are always safe places for learning and fun, no matter where they are. Everyone listened very carefully and with kind hearts. When sad things happen, it's very good that important people listen. Listening is the first step to understanding, and understanding is the first step to helping and making things better. Did you know? Many people around the world believe that every child has a right to be safe, to be loved, and to go to school to learn and grow!

Keeping Children Safe, Everywhere!

The people at the United Nations heard her important message. They know that children, everywhere in the world, deserve to be safe. It doesn't matter if you live in a big city in Africa, a small village in Asia, or a town far away across the ocean, you deserve a safe school. It is so important that children can learn and play without any worries. Schools should always be places where happy memories are made. They should be places where you can laugh loudly with your friends and discover new things every day. They are places where you can dream big, big dreams for your future. People from different countries are always trying to work together. They want to make sure that all schools are safe for all children, all the time. They are trying to find solutions so no child has to face danger at school. Did you know? Your safety is important, no matter where you live! We all want you to be happy and safe as you grow up.

Working Together for a Brighter Future

Even though this story starts with something very sad, it also shows us something very, very good and hopeful. It shows us that many, many people care about children. It shows us that when something bad happens, people come together to try and make things better for everyone. The brave mother spoke up for her children and for all children, and important people listened with their hearts. They are trying to find ways to keep all schools safe, all the time, for every child. They are working hard so that every child can go to school feeling happy and secure, ready to learn and play. We all want a world where children can laugh, learn, and grow up safely, with lots of love and joy. We can all help by being kind to each other, listening to grown-ups, and by thinking about how we can make our own communities safer too. Did you know? When we work together, hand-in-hand, we can make amazing and wonderful changes in the world! Every little bit of kindness helps.
